rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DEPOSITION: $700.00 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Petitioner People of the State of California's motion for summary judgment is granted. The People's moving papers satisfy its summary judgment burden of showing that it is entitled to forfeiture of all the property sought to be forfeited to the State of California. Although he was served with the moving papers, claimant Jonathan Ratcliff has not filed any opposition papers, thereby requiring entry of summary judgment in favor of the People for the forfeiture of all the property sought to be forfeited to the State of California for this case. In addition to a proposed order on this motion, the People must submit a proposed judgment identifying all of the property that is forfeited. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALANNA ZABIELSKI VS. AIRBNB, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 3, PETITIONER ALANNA ZABEL's Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. Petitioner Alanna Zabel's first amended petition to vacate the arbitration award is denied. Zabel has not shown any of the limited grounds in the Federal Arbitration Act allowing for the vacatur of an arbitration award. Zabel's contention that the award should be vacated because the arbitrator failed to consider her evidence lacks merit. Courts distinguish between an arbitrator's consideration of evidence and the weight assigned to it. It is not a basis to vacate an arbitration award because the arbitrator declined to consider evidence he deemed legally irrelevant, even where that relevance determination is legally incorrect. (Schlessinger v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Susman (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1096, 1110-1111). Zabel's due process argument fares no better. An arbitrator is not required to conduct evidentiary hearings or afford witness testimony simply because a party requests it. Zabel's challenges to the arbitrator's procedural rulings are similarly unavailing. An arbitrator's authority to permit motions and manage hearing procedures falls squarely within the broad discretion afforded to the arbitrator. The arbitrator's decisions to postpone the hearing and deny the stay were within that authority. In short, Zabel has not established that any of the three grounds asserted in her first amended petition warrants vacating the arbitration award. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: SAYURI MARTIN VS. SAM FINI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Reopen Discovery To Take The Deposition Of Andrew Martin
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 4, DEFENDANTS SAM FINI, AMERISF FINANCIAL GROUP INC., and KUMIKO BRUNET's Motion for Leave to Reopen Discovery To Take The Deposition Of Andrew Martin. Defendants' motion to reopen discovery to take the deposition of Andrew Martin is granted on the condition that defendants agree that Mr. Martin may remain as the attorney for plaintiff and defendants will not seek to have Mr. Martin disqualified or argue that he should be disqualified. Defendants may signify their assent to this condition by submitting a proposed order that includes language that they so assent. If defendants agree to this condition, there is no legal prejudice to the plaintiff by the granting of this motion and non-expert discovery will now close on May 11, 2026 for the sole purpose of enabling defendants to take the deposition of Mr. Martin. If defendants do not assent to the condition on the granting of this motion, the motion is denied and reasonable monetary sanctions of $1,500 must be paid by defendants to plaintiff no later than May 11, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 16, PLAINTIFF SARAH LEWIS'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiff Sarah Lewis' motion to quash subpoenas is granted as to the subpoena issued to AirBnb and granted in part as to the four subpoenas issued to various UCSF entities. Defendant Luis Magallon has not provided any basis for the subpoena to AirBnB. By using the phrase "including but not limited to" all four subpoenas issued to the UCSF entities are overbroad and violate Lewis's privacy by seeking all documents pertaining to medical care provided to Lewis during the specified time period rather than seeking documents only pertaining to the medical care provided by the physicians listed in Lewis' response to special interrogatory 1. The four subpoenas must be revised to seek only records from the physicians listed in Lewis' response to special interrogatory 1. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: DAVID GUTEKUNST VS. LINDSAY BRENNAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order (Requests For Production), And Request For Sanctionsand Notice Of Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 5, 2- PLAINTIFF DAVID GUTEKUNST's Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order (Requests For Production), And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff David Gutekunst's motion to compel compliance with court order regarding set one requests for production for documents served by plaintiff is denied. This motion highlights an apparent difficulty in the ability of plaintiff's counsel and defendant Leighton Burrey to effectively communicate with each other. The court credits Burrey's statement made under penalty of perjury that the only two documents withheld on the grounds of privilege from his production in response to the set one document requests are reflected in his privilege log dated February 19, 2026. Had Burrey explained to plaintiff's counsel why the supplemental privilege log reflects fewer documents withheld on the grounds of privilege than his prior privilege log that may have obviated plaintiff's felt need to file this motion. Regardless, once Burrey's statement is credited, this motion must be denied because there is nothing to require Burrey to do to comply with the court order regarding set one requests for production of documents that he hasn't already done. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: DAVID GUTEKUNST VS. LINDSAY BRENNAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order (Special Interrogatories), And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 5, 1- PLAINTIFF DAVID GUTEKUNST's Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order (Special Interrogatories), And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff David Gutekunst's motion to compel compliance with court order regarding set one special interrogatories served by plaintiff is granted in part. No later than May 11, 2026 Burrey must serve verified further responses to special interrogatories 14 and 15 stating whether he objects to all information sought by those interrogatories based on attorney-client privilege and the attorney work product doctrine and, if not, to provide all non-privileged responsive information. Defendant Leighton Burrey initially responded to special interrogatories 14 and 15 by objecting that information sought by those interrogatories is protected by the attorney-client privilege and the attorney work product doctrine, but not stating whether all or only some of the responsive information is so protected. Because Burrey did not provide further code-compliant responses to interrogatories 14 and 15 as required by the January 20, 2026 order, he must pay $1,500 in reasonable monetary sanctions to plaintiff by May 11, 2026 for the reasonable expenses to file this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: DESIRAE FERIA VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Unified School District To Provide Further Responses, Without Objection, To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 8, PLAINTIFF DESIRAE FERIA'S Motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Unified School District To Provide Further Responses, Without Objection, To Special Interrogatories. Plaintiff Desirae Feria's motion to compel defendant San Francisco Unified School District to provide further responses to set six special interrogatories is denied as to interrogatories 212 and 217 and granted as to interrogatories 218-221. The contact information for current SFUSD employees is not discoverable because those employees may be contacted through defense counsel. The requested information for the four persons who reported disabilities to SFUSD who were supervised directly or indirectly by Michelle Zapata is discoverable because those persons are within the possible persons who have discoverable "me too" information. SFUSD must serve further verified responses consistent with this order no later than May 13, 2026. Both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Both sides acted with substantial justification regarding the issues raised by this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC ET AL VS. ARI LAW, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT AALAEI ALI AND NILOFAR TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT (ADDED PER THE COURT'S 4/21/26 RULING)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 17, DEFENDANT AALAEI ALI AND NILOFAR TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT (ADDED PER THE COURT'S 4/21/26 RULING). Defendant Aalaei Ali and Nilofar Trust's motion to strike the complaint is granted without leave to amend and this case is dismissed. All six causes of action alleged by plaintiffs are related to the claims alleged in the case 23CV425115 pending in Santa Clara Superior Court and are therefore compulsory cross-claims which were required by CCP 426.30(a) to be filed in 23CV425115 rather than as a separate lawsuit in this court. (Currie Medical Specialties, Inc. v. Bowen (1982) 136 Cal.App.3d 774, 777 ("The waiver provision of section 426.30 is mandatory, the policy in favor of hearing all related claims in a single action controlling.")) The fact that there are different parties in this case than in 23CV425115 does not make section 426.30 inapplicable. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW SHIREY VS. MARIA SHEVCHENKO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, April 27, 2026, Line 9, PLAINTIFF ANDREW SHIREY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission. Hearing required. Due to plaintiff serving the same or similar discovery on both defendants, the relationship between the defendants and the service of discovery responses by the individual defendant after this motion was filed, which defendants apparently contend provide plaintiff with all the information he is seeking by the discovery at issue on this motion, the court is unable to determine without additional assistance from the parties whether any or all of the issues raised by this motion (apart from document requests other than requests 25 and 26) have been mooted by the individual defendant's discovery responses. Moreover, defendants' failure to file an opposition separate statement further complicates the task of ruling on this motion. The court will seek assistance from the parties as to the most efficient manner to address the parties' discovery disputes, which might entail an additional meet and confer session in light of the recent discovery responses and/or the convening of an informal discovery conference. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
